Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Laboratory results are overall within normal limits, with good glycemic control and preserved renal function. Mildly elevated chloride and slightly decreased total CO₂ may suggest mild metabolic acidosis or compensation, but without other abnormalities, this is likely not clinically significant. Lipid profile and urine microalbumin/creatinine ratio are unremarkable. No acute concerns from a laboratory perspective; recommend clinical correlation.
